course_meta.notes: Title inferred from context; exact course code/credits not provided in excerpt.

learning_outcomes: Introduce concepts related to general anatomy, muscular, respiratory, circulatory, digestive, and excretory systems.
Demonstrate and understand the basic anatomy of the respiratory and circulatory systems.
Demonstrate and understand the basic anatomy of the digestive and excretory systems.
Gain knowledge of the basic concepts of human body anatomical structure.
Understand interrelationships, gross, functional, and applied anatomy of various structures in the human body.
higher_order_objectives: Analyse the respiratory system with bronchopulmonary segments and the circulatory system including types of blood vessels, heart, and pericardium.
Assess the digestive system, role of digestive juices and enzymes, and the reproductive system including spermatogenesis and oogenesis.
Formulate the excretory system pathway of glomerular filtration rate with structure of nephrons.

units.title: Introduction, Musculoskeletal and Nervous Systems
topics: Introduction to anatomy, anatomical terms, planes, organization of human body: cell, tissue, organ, organ system.
Musculoskeletal system: types of bones; structure and divisions of the skeletal system; names of bones and their parts; joints classification; structure and types of muscles.
Anatomy of the nervous system: central and peripheral nervous systems and their components.

units.title: Circulatory and Respiratory Systems
topics: Anatomy of circulatory system: general plan and components.
Heart: size, location, coverings, chambers, blood supply, nerve supply; general plan of circulation; pulmonary circulation.
Names and positions of major arteries and veins; lymphatic system general plan.
Anatomy of the respiratory system: organs, parts, and positions (brief).

units.title: Digestive and Excretory Systems (Part 1)
topics: Anatomy of the alimentary tract and its parts.
Accessory glands of digestion: pancreas, liver, gall bladder.
Anatomy of the excretory system: kidneys (location, gross structure), excretory ducts, ureters, urinary bladder, urethra.

units.title: Endocrine System
topics: Names and positions of all endocrine glands.
Hormones and functions: pituitary, thyroid, parathyroid, adrenal glands, gonads, islets of pancreas.

practicals: Demonstration of parts of microscope and its uses.
Demonstration of deltoid and gluteus maximus, cubital fossa.
